extra.reasoning: The invariant (opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable states. Proof by induction over transitions: Initial state has opted_out=false, sent=false, so the invariant holds vacuously. The only transition that sets sent:=true is [t2] (on send_attempt), guarded by "not opted_out"; it leaves opted_out=false, so the invariant is preserved. opted_out is monotonic: only [t1] writes it (always to true), and no transition ever resets it to false. [t1] sets opted_out:=true but simultaneously sets sent:=false, so the post-state has opted_out=true, sent=false (invariant holds). [t3] (send_attempt when opted_out) sets sent:=false. [t4] (tick) sets sent:=false unconditionally. Thus every transition either keeps sent=false whenever opted_out is true, or sets sent=true only while opted_out is false. Since opted_out can never revert to false, once it is true no enabled transition can make sent true (the only sent:=true transition [t2] is disabled when opted_out is true, and [t3] fires instead setting sent:=false). Therefore no reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true, and the global property holds for all executions (including up to 10 events).
